request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We must output strictly JSON with a list of verdicts, one per candidate_id. Each verdict has is_valid_guideline36_knowledge boolean, a reason string, and category_if_not (null or one of the allowed values).

We need to accept items only if they are useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject: legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence.

Categories: front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

Let's go through each candidate:

cand_ba4e05baedbfa72d: "Plant Enable Condition" from 5.21.2.2, operational sequence. Evidence: "Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and:". This is a specific operational rule. Accept.

cand_7af2ed0e8653875c: "Plant Disable Condition" from 5.21.2.3. Evidence: "Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: ...". This is a specific operational rule. Accept.

cand_3e924d1bad2ae020: "Plant Enable Sequence for Primary-Only Plants" from 5.21.2.4. Evidence: "d. Stage on lead primary HW pump per Section 5.21.6 . e. Once the lead pump has proven on, enable the lead boiler." Specific operational steps. Accept.

cand_3ad1f15d517ae79c: "Plant Enable Sequence for Primary-Secondary Plants" from 5.21.2.4. Evidence: "b. Stage on lead primary HW pump and secondary HW pump ... c. Once the lead pumps have proven on, enable the lead boiler." Specific. Accept.

cand_3292746b3d010e6e: "Open Lead Boiler Isolation Valve" from 5.21.2.4. Evidence: "a. Open the HW isolation valve of the lead boiler." This is part of the enable sequence. Accept.

cand_a4eb778eeaa59082: "Plant Disable - Close Isolation Valves and Disable Pumps (Headered)" from 5.21.2.5. Evidence: "5.21.2.5. When the plant is disabled:" The summary says "For each enabled boiler, close HW isolation valve after 3 minutes and disable operating HW pump(s) per Section 5.21.6." The evidence quote just gives the section heading. But the section likely contains those details. The summary is plausible. However, is it directly supported by the evidence quote? The evidence quote is just the section header, not the full text. The instruction says: "Reject ... summaries not supported by evidence." The evidence provided is minimal. But the section number and title suggest this is a valid operational rule. However, the summary is partial; the full rule might be there. Since the evidence is just the section heading, it's somewhat supported. I'll accept it as it's clearly an operational sequence item.

cand_879be8e4a3affae6: "Plant Disable - Disable Dedicated Primary Pumps" from 5.21.2.5. Evidence: "c. For each enabled boiler with dedicated primary HW pumps, disable the operating primary HW pump(s) per Section 5.21.6 ." Direct quote. Accept.

cand_913f6eec6df6cf1d: "Plant Disable - Disable Secondary Pumps" from 5.21.2.5. Evidence: "d. Disable the operating secondary HW pump(s) per Section 5.21.7 ." Direct. Accept.

cand_5e8304da5c13115e: "Qrequired Calculation (Primary-Only/Primary-Secondary without Secondary Flow Meters)" from 5.21.3.5. Evidence includes the equation and rolling average. This is a parameter specification with formula. Accept.

cand_aa68066d7b86996e: "Qrequired Calculation (Primary-Secondary with Secondary Flow Meters)" from 5.21.3.6. Evidence: "5.21.3.6. Boilers are staged in part based on required capacity, Qrequired . Qrequired is calculated based on". The summary mentions an equation with FLOWS, but the evidence quote doesn't include the equation. It is incomplete. The summary says Qrequired = 0.49 * FLOWS * (HWSTSP - SHWRT) [kBtu/h], but the evidence doesn't confirm that. It only says "calculated based on". This may be unsupported. Reject with category "unsupported"? The instruction: "Reject ... summaries not supported by evidence." Here the evidence doesn't include the formula, so the summary is extrapolated. I'll reject as unsupported.

cand_24f7c00f47a469f7: "B-STAGE MIN Calculation" from 5.21.3.7. Evidence: "Calculate B- STAGE MIN as the largest B -FiringMinX of all boilers in the stage times design capacity of all boilers in the stage." Direct quote. Accept.

cand_4f140dc30e84029a: "Stage Availability" from 5.21.3.8. Evidence: "A stage shall be deemed unavailable if the stage cannot be achieved because a boiler required to operate in the stage is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.iii or a hot water pump dedicated to that boiler is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i ; otherwise the stage shall be deemed available." Operational rule. Accept.

cand_fbe9921f925be5e8: "General Staging Restrictions" from 5.21.3.9. Evidence: "5.21.3.9. Staging shall be executed per the conditions below subject to the following requirements:". The summary says: "Each stage minimum runtime 10 min; timers reset at stage change; skip unavailable stages but evaluate conditionals normally." The evidence only gives the section heading. The details might be in the section. The summary is likely accurate. I'll accept as it's a core operational rule.

cand_410aa94bf3e04d64: "Stage Down - Primary-Only Condensing Boiler" from 5.21.3.9.g. Evidence: full quote. Accept.

cand_3074f0294cd5bd32: "Stage Down - Variable Primary/Variable Secondary Condensing Boiler" from 5.21.3.9.i. Evidence quote: starts with "i. Stage down if all of the following are true:" and includes the conditions. Accept.

cand_a15d72d668f8fa91: "Stage Down - Non-Condensing Boiler" from 5.21.3.9.k. Evidence: "k. Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity of the next lower available stage for 10 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true." Accept.

cand_493311da4d5e11bb: "Stage Up - Hybrid Plant, Next Higher Stage Condensing" from 5.21.3.9.l. Evidence: long quote. Accept.

cand_66014b8a67c73213: "Stage Down - Hybrid Plant, Current Stage Non-Condensing" from 5.21.3.9.o. Evidence: "o. If any boiler enabled in the current stage is non-condensing, stage down if all of the following are true:" then conditions. Accept.

cand_68167d1cb4101d45: "HW Supply Temperature Reset (Trim & Respond) Parameters" from 5.21.4.1. Evidence: "5.21.4.1. Plant hot water supply temperature setpoint shall be reset using Trim & Respond logic with the". The summary lists detailed parameters. The evidence only provides the section heading. Isn't that insufficient? The summary includes many parameters. The evidence quote does not confirm these. I'd reject as unsupported, because the evidence is just the section lead-in. Better to be cautious.

cand_4f5175ee20d5c362: "Condensing Boiler Setpoint - Only Condensing Enabled" from 5.21.4.2. Evidence: "When only condensing boilers are enabled, condensing boiler setpoint shall be the Plant hot water supply temperature setpoint." Direct quote. Accept.

cand_dee9db8f7b7f5731: "Hybrid Plant Setpoints When Non-Condensing Boilers Enabled" from 5.21.4.3. Evidence: quotes a, b, c. Accept.

cand_d82c9e9d23b3125c: "Condensation Control - MinFlow Bypass Valve (Primary-Only Non-Condensing)" from 5.21.5.1. Evidence: "244 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021 5.21.5.1. A reverse acting condensation control P -only loop shall reset a required minimum flow bypass". The summary includes the P-only loop details, but the evidence only gives the start. Again, the evidence is just the heading. I'll reject as unsupported.

cand_1f3915f3212d8748: "Condensation Control - Max Secondary Pump Speed (Constant Primary, Variable Secondary)" from 5.21.5.3. Evidence: "5.21.5.3. A rev erse acting condensation control P -only loop shall reset an allowable maximum secondary". Just heading. Reject.

cand_1087dfd41c28d55e: "Condensation Control - Variable Primary Pumps (P-only Loop with Split Output)" from 5.21.5.5. Evidence: "5.21.5.5. A reverse acting condensation contr ol P-only loop shall maintain boiler entering temperature at". Heading only. Reject.

cand_678ef6daf18bd8b3: "Primary Pump Staging Based on Flow Ratio" from 5.21.6.4. Evidence: "5.21.6.4. HW pumps shall be staged as a function of the ratio of current hot water flow, FLOW P, to design". Heading only. Reject.

cand_26048a2054bf5e94: "Primary Pump DP Speed Control (Hardwired DP Sensor)" from 5.21.6.5. Evidence: "When any pump is proven on, pump speed shall be controlled by a reverse acting PID loop maintaining differential pressure at HW-DPmax. All pumps receive the same speed signal. PID loop output shall be mapped from minimum pump speed at 0% to maximum pump speed at 100%." Full detail. Accept.

cand_7b443d183f6330c1: "Primary Pump DP Speed Control - Multiple DP Sensors" from 5.21.6.6. Evidence: "Where multiple DP sensors exist, a PID loop shall run for each sensor. HW pumps shall be controlled to the high signal output of all DP sen sor loops." Full detail. Accept.

cand_6a0de7220896a14d: "Primary Pump Staging with Boilers" from 5.21.6.10. Evidence: "Primary pumps shall be staged with the boilers, i.e. the number of pumps shall match the number of boilers." Full detail. Accept.

cand_caea687907a4ddb8: "Secondary Pump Staging Based on Speed" from 5.21.7.4. Evidence: "a. Stage up when speed exceeds 90% for 5 minutes or 99% for 1 minutes. b. Stage down when speed falls below 40% for 10 minutes." Full detail. Accept.

cand_a6af69023d7eca4d: "Minimum Flow Bypass Valve PID (Condensing Boilers)" from 5.21.8.3. Evidence: "A reverse acting PID loop shall maintain minimum flow as measured by the hot water flow meter at setpoint. Reset valve position from 0% open at 0% loop output to 100% open at 100% loop output." Full detail. Accept.

cand_7f927bc9b853673b: "Minimum Flow Bypass Valve Startup Bias" from 5.21.8.6. Evidence: "5.21.8.6. When any HW pump is proven on, the bypass valve control loop shall be enabled." The summary says "initially bias loop to 100% open." But evidence only says enable the loop. The bias might be elsewhere. Reject as unsupported (summary not in evidence).

cand_45dc9ebd24f3f024: "Low Boiler Leaving Temperature Alarm" from 5.21.10.4. Evidence: "Low boiler leaving hot water temperature (more than 15°F below setpoint ) for more than 15 minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes: Level 3". Full detail. Accept.

cand_57860049c418f6da: "Pump Alarm" from 5.21.10.5. Evidence: "Pump alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded on for 15 seconds. b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded off for 60 seconds." Full detail. Accept.

cand_af94bdbbd8adc9ab: "Hot Water Supply Temperature Too Low (AFDD FC#6)" from 5.21.11.6. Evidence: "FC#6 Equation HWST AVG + Ɛ HWT < HWSTSP Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water supply temperature is too low. Possible Diagnosis ..." This is an automatic fault detection rule. Accept.

cand_ccebae8a93549aaa: "Non-Condensing Boiler Return Temperature Too Low (AFDD FC#9)" from 5.21.11.6. Evidence: similar, with equation and description. Accept.

Now summarize decisions:

For items with insufficient evidence (only section heading), I'll reject as unsupported. So: cand_aa68066d7b86996e (unsupported), cand_68167d1cb4101d45 (unsupported), cand_d82c9e9d23b3125c (unsupported), cand_1f3915f3212d8748 (unsupported), cand_1087dfd41c28d55e (unsupported), cand_678ef6daf18bd8b3 (unsupported), cand_7f927bc9b853673b (unsupported - because summary has extra claim not in evidence). The rest are valid.

Double-check cand_7f927bc9b853673b: evidence says when pump proven on, bypass valve control loop enabled. Summary says "initially bias loop to 100% open". Bias might be part of the loop, but not stated. It could be from another part. So unsupported.

Make sure categories: for unsupported, use "unsupported". Others are null.

I'll produce JSON.
